reviews.review_description: This game was starting off good, but then it went bad, I was playing with my cousin on multiplayer and we tried to kill zombies but they wouldn’t die, also whenever we died, my cousin was kicked from the experience, so this made the game long and boring.

Ok...first off,hahahahahaha! Secondly,Pokemon and chakras,ahahaha! It's terrifying but also funny...but did I mention it's terrifying...to hear the dead ramble on a bout everyday things,adds a creep factor other games haven't nailed,and that black gun smoke adds to the lvl of intensity when the lights are out and you have to use 🔦 this game is perfect,I hope to see more buildings in the future,hopefully mansion like with doors you must pull and can close maybe? Love the weapon system and lvl system,perfect! 6 out of 5 stars. Amazingly great price too,most games the firearm or weapon is off and my brain knows where my hand is irl,so it always itches me,but you guys did awesome.

Sick game  and creepy as hell. 
Runs flawless. Very intense.
This is the best game in this genre. Maybe the best game in the meta store. Nothing to compare it to. 
Graphics and love for details are amazing.
Same for the sound.
Endless replayability!!

Agree with all the great reviews!

Feels like a zombie action movie!


Great Job!

The addition of a story mode with more buildings to fight your way thru would be a nice addition in the future such as exiting buildings and make your way thru alley ways to other buildings or sewer ways….   

Added weapons 

I would pay for the added content!

I’ll  reserve  a five star review for future updates

Updated Review: Hours in and still really enjoying this game. It's simple, intense, and a whole load of fun. 

Any updates coming our way?

reviews.review_title: Amazing game but cant turn yourself
reviews.review_description: I love the game but it wont allow me to turn any direction only back and forward and I have to recalibrate my play area to be able to adjust myself.

developer_response.body: there is in options: smooth or snap turning. ground floor. its off initially for you to enjoy turning your head and your body for increased realism

reviews.review_description:
Claustrophobic, cramped, overwhelmingly noisy, and probably the most stressful game I've played in ages. Most games rely on complex mechanics and broad content to keep things interesting, this by comparison is simple - 1 zombie type and gun, just you and 31 floors of increasingly fast and numerous zombies.

But oftentimes when all the parts are done right, basic is king. This never gets boring, and that's why this game is phenomenal, so few 'moving parts' but everything is so tightly formed.

Brings me the very same feels of when I played Doom 93 for the first time, so immediate and visceral. But this is by far, much more terrifying. Had plenty of 10/10 jumpscares in ways most horror games just fall flat. It's absolute hell, I love it.

Highly recommend this game.
Ngl this game lokey fire asf but I have a suggestion you should add ragdoll and more guns
You should add multiplayer

I gave the trial a shot, and was hooked after about 10 minutes.  This is probably the most addictive and intense zombie shooter I've ever played.  You've got 2 unlimited ammo rapid fire guns, a flashlight if and when needed, and 31 floors of zombie mayhem to get through.  The only problem is that if you get bit once, you die, and have to start from floor 1 again.  But the good thing is that you gain experience points as you kill zombies, which you can then use to power up your guns, movement speed, etc.
Your guns can overheat if you fire them for too long, so short controlled bursts and head shots are required to get through the chaos.  As long as you don't die, you can quit the game after any cleared floor and then resume the next time you decide to play.  To give you an idea of how many zombies are around, I've killed 1700 of them after 6 floors, and they do start moving faster as you progress.
All I can say is that for a lower priced zombie shooter, this is the best one I've played, and it makes me feel like John Wick in an action-packed zombie movie.  :)

reviews.review_title: THE BEST VERY REALISTIC ZOMBIE GAME
reviews.review_description: WUAOOO great, a very realistic game, good images, I can already imagine if a PART 2 comes out with a better story and better graphics, CONGRATULATIONS TO THE CREATORS

This is early excess (7/13/25) so it naturally means it is not finished and has a lot of work to be done.   I really like the concept of you having to clear the zombies out of a building and have to clear all 31 floors!  One bite and you're dead. 

Each floor is a maze of rooms like a scary haunted house at Halloween.  The shapes and sizes are not the normal. Hallways are not straight. Some rooms have zombies and some don't.  You have to find your way through the maze to where the zombies are.  Then kill them (head shots only) and get back to the lobby of the building before time runs out.  When it runs out, the lights go out and now you are hunting in pitch black.  You have a flashlight in one hand but if you do, that is one less gun you can shoot with.  Without a flashlight, the ONLY light is from the flash from the muzzle when you shoot.  If you shoot too much, too fast, the guns get hot and shut down until they cool down... 

This is truly an intense, dark, scary game but still needs a lot of work.

Things I would change/fix/do.  

1) Any button your press (either controller) will bring up your stat sheet telling you how many zombies you have left to clear the floor. I would make the B and or Y buttons only for this. You really don't need to see the stat sheet that much, it's not that important to game play.

2) The changing from gun to flashlight and back is confusing and sometimes doesn't work well.  You sometimes have to go all the way back down to the lobby, get out of the elevator, change it, then go back up.  I would make the A & X buttons responsible for switching the gun to flashlight and back on each controller. 

3) You find zombies by listening to them talk. It would be better if they just moaned and groaned periodically.  Making your search harder because where they are would not be as easy to figure out.
You are clearing out a building of zombies then the lights go out with nothing to see but your muzzle flash.nice graphics no complaints with controls.

It’s like that Sound of Music meme, pirouetting with a couple tec-9s.

It reminds me of a PlayStation game called Loaded, which was a top down shooter set in an insane asylum. 

Four stars for losing that old description. When they start biting people is the only time they notice. Restore it to its former glory and I’ll change it to five stars.

Sometimes you don’t need a game that has a story, great characters, mechanics, etc. Sometimes you just need a very simple game to release all of your stress from work or life in general. THIS GAME DELIVERS! Levels upon levels of just non stop zombie slaughtering with guns that you can upgrade and DON’T have to worry about reloading, besides letting them cool off. It’s a race against time however as the lights turn off after 5 minutes! 😱 but no worries to keep the lights on, all you gotta is keep killing! I do have to give the developers a one zonkwith how you move inside the game. Otherwise great game for the price!
Best zombie game so far
Feels like doom and cod zombies put together. Very intense when the lights go out and you still have to kill 100 zombies, only being able to see them with the flash of the guns. Insane!
Great game can’t wait for future content.

It's fun , and the replayability value is high if it gets updates . I'm playing on quest 2 and it's still very good , I'm sure it's better on q3, but I haven't upgraded so it's nice we still get games like this on quest 2.
RP as a police officer in a traphouse  with a horde of geeked out crackheads, and things get pretty interesting..

This game is so good... Tip... At first stay close to the
 elevator
I spent 3 dollars on this expecting something pretty subpar. This game is freaking great. It can be overwhelming and satisfying at the same time. Great "house of the dead" kind of game

reviews.review_title: Get This Thing Away From Me!!
reviews.review_description:
Bought this game several months ago, and it was fun to dip into but obviously still a work in progress.
Well, since the latest updates, this has become something really special!
It's objective is very simple: clear each floor of zombies and ascend to the next, where there are even more zombies, and if you don't kill them fast enough, you have to do it in the dark with only the muzzle-flash of your pistols to guide you.
But there are A LOT of zombies. And they are truly hideous! They look wet and disgusting, and they surge out of the confined spaces at you in a slavering, seathing horde, hissing and gargling at you and lunging for your jugular.
If you want to feel completely overwhelmed by visceral, blood-curdling, unrelenting horror, then this is a must have. More people should be aware of this game, as it's the most intense experience I've had to date--and I've been playing since Oculus Quest One.
Graphically it's very good, as is the lighting, and the sound design. And the gameplay is beautifully simple, but very, very intense. A true five star experience hands down. Can't recommend this one enough.
Try it and you'll be bitten.

Definitely a must have for anyone looking for a truly horrific, zombie ridden atmosphere that leaves you with an overwhelming sense of fear & uncertainty. I admit I didn’t think much of it at the time from the store page screenshots, but once I gave it a try I was absolutely terrified, yet amazed by the way the zombies move and get around corners. The zombies are incredibly unpredictable and always left me figuring out which direction they were coming from, the zombies even manage to jumpscare you in certain situations when you approach them at a long corridor by picking up speed and pretty much running at you or popping out from some corner.

When zombies grab in an attempt to kill the player, they’ll lean down towards your face in order to bite you resulting in a game over if you let them hold onto you for too long. Not to mention how hopeless you may feel when the power goes out on the current floor during a run when everything goes black. Most of the rooms are barely lit or use colored light fixtures like red, orange, green, blue or violet that really add a certain aesthetic.

Plus it recently got a new update adding a nice introduction screen with total post-game zombie kills statistic at the end of every run

Hands down the most terrifying vr game I have
 ever played. Bravo to the developer for achieving horror perfection in the use of vr as a medium. Probably too hardcore for the official store. If you came here out of curiosity, trust me, it's as scary as you think it is. Just buy it.

The developer is very nice! I reported that bullet detection was awful and it has been fixed, thanks! Still not perfect, zombies should bleed or something when hit any zone apart from head. On the other hand, are there more weapons? Zombie dogs, fat guys, etc? I'd be great to add more variety. Also a quick reload with side buttons would be cool. And an option to put background music, choose between a metal or rechno track would be simple but cool. A good game with still a lot potential to be unleashed! 😀  Thanks!

Good fun game, nice graphics. A few more weapons and a sitting mode would be nice.
Game gets a bit repetitive but still fun.

reviews.review_title: Hands down the best game on oculus
reviews.review_description: Amazing! Though i am a bit biased since I made it. Thanks

reviews.review_title: Not bad with graphics and sound design
reviews.review_description: The graphics and sound design is amazing on this game with gun whistling when you shoot a far target from the very nice and realistic textures. The only issue this game has is its repetitiveness with each floor, each floor tons of zombies that you have to kill with your two TEC-9's which sounds like fun and is fun for a little under an hour at least. Every single level is the same repetitive murder spree over and over again with the only change being the number of zombies and they're walk speed, though this is still pretty good for $10 and its pretty good for being applab aswell.

The positives: The developers have nailed the atmosphere. Dark, gloomy and a zombie coming round the corner genuinely makes you jump. Graphics are good, although more varied zombies would be nice.

The negatives: Variety is the biggest problem here. There' are 30 odd floors/levels and the only difference I've noticed between the levels are that the zombies move faster. 

The flashing strobe effect when the guns fire will give someone a seizure, I'm certain of it!!!

This is early access, so hopefully there's more to come. As I've said they've nailed the atmosphere, so if they can build from that then this game has potential!!

reviews.review_title: Scary, original, and also kinda funny
reviews.review_description:
The scariest VR zombie game I’ve played yet. Nice graphics, always got freaked out when the lights turned off, and creepy atmosphere 

Also The zombie and walking animation all about it is straight up original like normally VR zombie games I get the zombies are just the zombie assets that you find in crappy zombie games and also the animations are usually from Miximo but yours actually has its own unique zombie and animations

Lastly sometimes when the zombie can’t find you they will act like they’re drunk or they will be acting like they’re a robot dancing which is priceless ￼
Solid game with nice graphics

That game is so cool, there’s no time to relax or calm down, you gotta all the zombies behind or in your front, and this makes the game incredible and perfect to me.

GREAT JOB 👌

Very exciting and scary zombie game! However being  able 
to save your progress would be a great benefit. Also, maybe upgrade the weapons, be able to push the zombies, or pistol whip them would make the game even that much more exciting.
. One more thing! Multiplayer would be awesome.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review and suggestions... The game saves 
automatically

reviews.review_description:
I like this game, especially the concept. I want to like it more but I seem to be having an issue that my bullets aren't working. I empty a clip into one zombie and it's like nothing happened. I'm not sure if it's something I'm doing or an issue with the game. Nobody else seems to be having the issue.
The atmosphere, sounds, and gunplay is great. Not having to reload is nice but also something I have mixed feelings about because reloading is a big part of immersion. It seems like a bloddy mess of a good time, I'm just unable to go anywhere do to my bullet tracking issue. Hopefully I can figure out what I'm doing wrong.

developer_response.body: it does work. get closer and shoot the head

reviews.review_description:
Very fun and satisfying gameplay. Surprisingly frightening experience. Maybe the sounds or the way they move. I catch myself being too afraid to explore sometimes. You don’t want to get caught in the middle. I usually wait for them to come to me in the beginning until the herd thins out.

Sure there could be more variety overall. 
More weapons. Weapon upgrades. Maybe running, pushing or pistol whip :)
And totally charge more $ for the game

For the price it’s an awesome VR experience and I will be excited for any future updates

Wow this game is awesome !!!

Here is where it all started just entering that elevator. When I made it to the 1st floor and those elevator doors opened just the gloomy environment and the sound I was already scared and thought nope I am definitely not gonna make it...  Then I realised as I went around the corner and saw rooms full of zombies and  they spotted me immediately they headed straight for me.
There were times I thought I finished off a room and the next thing I knew I was being attacked from behind or I saw their creepy hand wrapped around the door frame...  
While playing I realised at that very moment I was the last human being during this zombie  apocalypse.
